title: Neuronal Migration Disorders: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Neuronal Migration Disorders
Identify key diagnostic features of Neuronal Migration Disorders
Apply clinical reasoning to Neuronal Migration Disorders management
Recognize common pitfalls in Neuronal Migration Disorders

sections.title: Overview of Neuronal Migration Disorders
sections.content:
**Neuronal Migration Disorders**

Lissencephaly, polymicrogyria, heterotopia, schizencephaly; timing and mechanisms

**Key Points:**
• Neuronal migration occurs weeks 12-24 of gestation
• Lissencephaly (smooth brain): failed migration → absent gyri (agyria) or reduced gyri (pachygyria); LIS1, DCX genes
• Polymicrogyria: excessive small gyri from late migration/organization defect

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Neuronal migration occurs weeks 12-24 of gestation
• Lissencephaly (smooth brain): failed migration → absent gyri (agyria) or reduced gyri (pachygyria); LIS1, DCX genes

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Polymicrogyria: excessive small gyri from late migration/organization defect
• Heterotopia: neurons stop before reaching cortex (periventricular nodular heterotopia — seizures, normal intelligence)
• Schizencephaly: full-thickness cleft lined by gray matter (open-lip vs closed-lip)

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Neuronal Migration Disorders:**

• Polymicrogyria: excessive small gyri from late migration/organization defect
• Heterotopia: neurons stop before reaching cortex (periventricular nodular heterotopia — seizures, normal intelligence)
• Schizencephaly: full-thickness cleft lined by gray matter (open-lip vs closed-lip)
